description: Safely quote compose override paths from harness.yaml/config.json across sandbox startup commands.

userStories.title: Centralize compose argument construction
userStories.description: As a harness maintainer, I want compose file and env-file arguments assembled by a shell-safe helper so every sandbox lifecycle entrypoint passes override paths literally.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a Bash executable helper that constructs Docker Compose arguments using arrays, not raw string concatenation, eval, or printed shell fragments for -f override paths.
Helper argument order is env files, base compose file, optional Hermes overlay, harness.yaml overlays, then config.json overlays.
Override paths keep repo-root-relative resolution and helper supports debug argv output without executing Docker Compose.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Wire Make targets through safe helper
userStories.description: As a harness user, I want Make lifecycle targets to use the same safe compose argument path.
acceptanceCriteria: Make lifecycle targets call the shared executable helper directly and no longer expand raw COMPOSE_OVERRIDES strings.
make config still prints generated harness env content when present and then runs docker compose config through the helper.
Target verbs and flags remain unchanged: sandbox uses up -d --build, destroy uses down -v, and other lifecycle targets keep their existing subcommands.
Protected Makefile is edited only by targeted diff; no deletion, rename, or deprecation.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Wire installer startup through safe helper
userStories.description: As a fresh installer, I want scripts/install.sh to use the same safe compose argument path when it builds and starts the sandbox.
acceptanceCriteria: Installer startup calls the shared helper rather than rebuilding COMPOSE_FILES with raw string concatenation.
Installer still writes the generated harness env file before compose execution and preserves existing GitHub-token and first-run messaging behavior.
Installer calls the helper from a defined repo-root context so relative override paths keep current behavior.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add compose override regression coverage
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want tests proving override paths with spaces and shell metacharacters remain single literal compose file arguments.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover harness.yaml and config.json override sources including paths with spaces and shell metacharacters.
Tests assert full literal argument ordering: env files, base, Hermes overlay, harness.yaml overlays, then config.json overlays.
Tests or static assertions verify Makefile and scripts/install.sh use the shared helper and no longer build raw compose override strings.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
